category_title: At-risk Grade 9 students exhibiting learned helplessness and inconsistent engagement
minimal_context: The student's small group is at the front of the room sharing their project with the whole class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for your small group to come up to the front and share your project with the whole class.

STUDENT: (Stands at the front of the class) Okay, here is our project. We organized it and put these parts in this order.

TUTOR: Go ahead and explain your work to everyone.

STUDENT: Well, we started with this... wait, why are they pointing at our project? I just heard someone say, 'well no, you can't put that there because...'

TUTOR: Just continue presenting your project to the class.

STUDENT: No, I'm done. Those kids love pointing out mistakes and they are making fun of us up here because we put something in the wrong order. I don't like being wrong.

options.A: Making sure that the group's presentation is accurate and meets the activity objectives.
options.B: Conferring privately with the student after class to discuss their project choices and concerns.
options.C: Encouraging the other students in the class to make funny suggestions about the project.
options.D: Responding by saying, "Another one already? No way! I’m so proud of you! Good job, keep going."
